content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (reflecting both light and one’s own thought processes) is taught as a core critical thinking skill for human high school learners, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be structured to mirror human language-based reflexive education curricula to improve generalizability. By grounding LLM self-reflection in the scaffolded, vocabulary-first framework human learners use to build reflexive critical thinking, we can help models move beyond niche self-reflection tricks to develop consistent, generalizable self-critique capabilities.
